conditions.label: Day 1 baseline (staff-provided)
conditions.description: qwen3-next-80b-a3b-thinking with no method improvements. Template email, no bench injection, no tone guard.

conditions.label: Ablation A — Signal-confidence only (no tone guard)
conditions.description: Signal-confidence-aware prompt + bench injection. Tone guard bypassed (_PASS_THRESHOLD=0). First LLM draft sent directly.

conditions.label: Ablation B — Tone guard only (no bench injection)
conditions.description: Tone guard active. Bench summary removed from system prompt. Signal-confidence-aware framing active.

conditions.label: Full method — Signal-confidence + bench injection + tone guard
conditions.description: All three layers active. llm_composer.py with full system prompt injection. tone_guard.enforce() with max_retries=2. Evaluated with Claude Sonnet 4.6 via OpenRouter, 1 trial per task on 30 held-out tasks.

delta_vs_baseline.note: Full method is 6.7pp below held-out baseline on τ²-Bench retail (not significant, n=30 underpowered). Expected: τ²-Bench retail tests generic agent capability, not Tenacious-specific constraints. Real differentiator is Tenacious-specific probe metrics below.

primary_finding: Real held-out evaluation (Claude Sonnet 4.6, 30 tasks, 1 trial each): pass@1 = 0.70, 6.7pp below held-out baseline (0.7667, n=30). Not significant (z=-0.584, p=0.72, n=30 underpowered — need ~440 for 80% power). τ²-Bench retail tests generic agent capability; it does not exercise Tenacious-specific bench injection or tone constraints. The mechanism's value is shown in Tenacious-specific probes: bench compliance +42pp (p<0.001), tone compliance +30pp (p=0.003). Held-out τ²-Bench result is honest and expected.
